goal_summary: Use a light, question-led sequence to recover from the clustered outreach on May 5 and get Maya to choose a short phone call with Andre.
lead_state_summary: Trailhead Catering Guild — Maya Esposito is a new potential lead created on May 2. On May 5, Andre sent three outbound SMS messages in the same day at 03:05, 13:39, and 14:12, plus an outgoing email at 14:12 with subject "contextual_follow_up." There is no recorded inbound reply, no workflow enrollment, and no event details yet, so the immediate need is to slow the cadence, acknowledge the prior outreach pattern indirectly by keeping the next touch simple, and ask one easy question that can open a conversation.
known_facts: Lead name is Trailhead Catering Guild — Maya Esposito.
An outgoing email was sent on 2026-05-05 at 14:12 with subject "contextual_follow_up."
Outbound SMS messages were sent on 2026-05-05 at 03:05, 13:39, and 14:12.
No inbound response is recorded in the Box.
Contact record includes one email and one phone number for Maya Esposito.
unknowns: Whether Maya is the right person for catering decisions.
What type of event Trailhead Catering Guild is planning.
Whether they are comparing multiple caterers right now.
Whether Maya prefers text, email, or a phone conversation.
Whether there is active interest or the lead source was exploratory.
best_next_question: Maya, when you looked into catering, what were you hoping to figure out first?

days.objective: Give space after the clustered May 5 outreach and prepare a cleaner next move.

required_actions.intent: Review the May 5 email and three SMS touches, then hold outbound for the rest of today unless Maya replies first.
required_actions.draft_seed: No send today. Last touch was email plus repeated SMS on May 5, so the next live message should be a single, low-pressure question tomorrow.
required_actions.notes: Avoid another same-day outbound because the Box already shows heavy contact today.

days.send_window: No outbound today; internal review during business hours.

days.objective: Re-open gently on SMS with one specific question that invites context.

required_actions.intent: Ask what Maya was trying to solve when she looked into catering.
required_actions.draft_seed: Maya, when Trailhead Catering Guild looked into catering, what were you hoping to figure out first?
required_actions.notes: Keep it to one question. No link, no pitch.

days.send_window: SMS between 10:00 AM and 1:00 PM lead-local time.

days.objective: If still no reply, use email to lower pressure and offer a short call as the easiest next step.

required_actions.intent: Send a brief reset email that references helping only if timing is real and offers a short call.
required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: Quick question

Maya — saw Trailhead Catering Guild come through and wanted to ask one simple thing: are you actively planning something right now, or was this more early research?

If it is active, Andre can do a quick 10-minute call and point you in the right direction.
required_actions.notes: Distinct intent from the SMS: clarify timing and readiness.

days.send_window: Email between 8:00 AM and 11:00 AM lead-local time.

days.objective: Have Andre personally review for any reply and be ready to convert interest into a scheduled call.

required_actions.intent: Review thread and, if Maya has replied with any event details or curiosity, send a personalized response aimed at booking a phone call.
required_actions.draft_seed: If Maya replies, answer her exact point first, then ask: Would it be easier to talk this through for 10 minutes with Andre?
required_actions.notes: No proactive outbound unless there is inbound. Keep under the weekly cap.

days.send_window: Internal review between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM lead-local time.

days.objective: Use one final SMS nudge that makes it easy to say yes, no, or not now.

required_actions.intent: Offer a simple forked choice to surface whether there is a live opportunity.
required_actions.draft_seed: Maya, is this something Trailhead Catering Guild is still exploring, or should Andre close the loop for now?
required_actions.notes: Designed to get a clean disposition or reopen conversation.

days.send_window: SMS between 11:00 AM and 3:00 PM lead-local time.

days.objective: Pause live outreach and prepare the call ask if Maya engages.

required_actions.intent: Monitor for response and prepare a short scheduling reply if Maya shows interest.
required_actions.draft_seed: If Maya responds positively, reply with two simple call options and ask which works better for her.
required_actions.notes: Do not send new outbound without an inbound signal.

days.objective: Send a final email that gives Maya a low-friction way to either book a call or decline.

required_actions.intent: Close the loop respectfully and offer a short phone conversation if timing is real.
required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: Should I keep this open?

Maya — I have reached out a few times since Trailhead Catering Guild came through, and I do not want to crowd you.

If catering is on the table, the easiest next step is a short call with Andre to understand date, headcount, and what you are trying to make happen.

If now is not the time, I can close the loop on my end.
required_actions.notes: Final touch for this cycle. Respectful tone, no pressure.

days.send_window: Email between 8:00 AM and 12:00 PM lead-local time.

stop_conditions.trigger: Maya replies with interest, event details, or asks a question.
Maya asks not to be contacted, says stop, remove me, or not interested.
Another team member manually enrolls Maya into a workflow or books a call.
